playArea.value:
In a double-slit experiment, a { $sourceType ->
  [photons] photon source
  [electrons] electron source
  [neutrons] neutron source
 *[heliumAtoms] helium atom source
} emits { $sourceType ->
  [photons] photons
  [electrons] electrons
  [neutrons] neutrons
 *[heliumAtoms] helium atoms
} toward a barrier with two narrow slits. { $sourceType ->
  [photons] Photons
  [electrons] Electrons
  [neutrons] Neutrons
 *[heliumAtoms] Helium atoms
} that pass through the slits land on a detector screen, building up a pattern over time. A magnified view of the slits, a front-facing detector screen, and an intensity graph are shown.

controlArea.value: Checkboxes show or hide a ruler and stopwatch. Play and pause controls and a Reset All button are available.

currentDetails.value:
Source is { $sourceType ->
  [photons] photons
  [electrons] electrons
  [neutrons] neutrons
 *[heliumAtoms] helium atoms
}. Slit configuration is { $slitSetting ->
  [bothOpen] both open
  [leftCovered] left covered
  [rightCovered] right covered
  [leftDetector] left detector
 *[rightDetector] right detector
}. Detection mode is { $detectionMode ->
  [averageIntensity] intensity
 *[hits] hits
}. { $isEmitting ->
  [true] { $detectionMode ->
    [hits] Source is on, { $totalHits } hits accumulated.
   *[averageIntensity] Source is on.
  }
 *[false] Source is off.
}

interactionHint.value:
{ $isEmitting ->
  [true] Change particle type, adjust slit settings, or switch detection mode to explore the experiment.
 *[false] Turn on the { $sourceType ->
    [photons] photon source
    [electrons] electron source
    [neutrons] neutron source
   *[heliumAtoms] helium atom source
  } to start the experiment.
}

accessibleParagraph.value:
{ $sourceType ->
  [electrons] Particle mass: 9.1 times 10 to the negative 31 kilograms.
  [neutrons] Particle mass: 1.7 times 10 to the negative 27 kilograms.
 *[heliumAtoms] Particle mass: 6.6 times 10 to the negative 27 kilograms.
}

accessibleName.value:
{ $sourceType ->
  [photons] Photon Source
  [electrons] Electron Source
  [neutrons] Neutron Source
 *[heliumAtoms] Helium Atom Source
}

accessibleHelpText.value: Turn the source on and off to start or stop the experiment.
Drag ruler up or down to align with features on the detector screen and graph.
Show or hide a ruler to measure distances on the detector screen.
Show or hide a stopwatch to time the experiment.
Adjust the distance between the centers of the two slits.
Adjust the distance from the barrier to the detector screen.

Adjust how many { $sourceType ->
  [photons] photons
  [electrons] electrons
  [neutrons] neutrons
 *[heliumAtoms] helium atoms
} the source emits per unit time.
Adjust the brightness of the detector screen display.
Choose how detections are displayed on the detector screen.

accessibleContextResponse.value: Detector screen shows continuous intensity pattern.

accessibleHelpText.value: Choose a particle type to change the source used in the double-slit experiment.

accessibleContextResponse.value: Experiment now uses a photon source.
Experiment now uses an electron source.
Experiment now uses a neutron source.
Experiment now uses a helium atom source.

accessibleHelpText.value: Choose a slit configuration to change which slits are open, covered, or monitored by a detector.

accessibleContextResponse.value:
{ $slitSetting ->
  [bothOpen] Both slits now open.
  [leftCovered] Left slit now covered.
  [rightCovered] Right slit now covered.
  [leftDetector] Left slit now monitored by a detector.
 *[rightDetector] Right slit now monitored by a detector.
}

accessibleParagraph.value:
Magnified view of the barrier with two vertical slits, each { $slitWidth } wide. { $slitSetting ->
  [bothOpen] Both slits are open.
  [leftCovered] Left slit is covered.
  [rightCovered] Right slit is covered.
  [leftDetector] Left slit is monitored by a which-path detector.
 *[rightDetector] Right slit is monitored by a which-path detector.
}

intensityOff.value: The graph is empty. The source is off.

intensity.value: The graph traces intensity versus position as a wave-like curve, rising to a tall central peak and falling through a series of smaller peaks on either side. { $spatialDescription }

intensitySingleSlit.value: The graph traces intensity versus position as a single broad peak centered on the screen, tapering smoothly toward the edges. { $spatialDescription }

hitsNone.value: The histogram is empty. No particles have been counted yet.

hitsFew.value: A few bars have appeared at scattered positions across the histogram. The distribution looks random so far.

hitsEmerging.value: The histogram is growing unevenly. Some positions are accumulating counts faster than others, hinting at a pattern beneath the randomness.

hitsDeveloping.value: The histogram is taking on a wave-like shape. Taller bars cluster at regular intervals with shorter bars between them. { $spatialDescription }

hitsClear.value: The histogram has settled into a stable wave-like shape matching the intensity curve, a central peak flanked by repeating side peaks, assembled from individual particle counts. { $spatialDescription }

hitsSingleSlitEmerging.value: The histogram is growing, with bars tallest near the center and shorter toward the edges.

hitsSingleSlitClear.value: The histogram shows a broad central peak that tapers toward the edges, matching the continuous intensity pattern. { $spatialDescription }

intensityOff.value: The detector screen is dark. The source is off.

intensity.value: The detector screen glows with alternating bright and dark vertical bands, strongest at the center and fading symmetrically outward. { $spatialDescription }

intensitySingleSlit.value: The detector screen glows brightest at the center, fading smoothly toward the edges in a broad diffraction pattern. { $spatialDescription }

hitsNone.value: The detector screen is empty. No particles have arrived yet.

hitsFew.value: A handful of particles have arrived, scattered across the screen in no obvious order.

hitsEmerging.value: As more particles arrive, a hint of order is appearing. Detections are beginning to cluster into faint vertical stripes with gaps between them.

hitsDeveloping.value: A pattern is emerging from the randomness. Individual impacts are gathering into distinct vertical bands, separated by regions where few particles land. { $spatialDescription }

hitsClear.value: A clear interference pattern has formed, one particle at a time. Dense vertical bands alternate with nearly empty gaps, the signature of wave-like behavior built from individual impacts. { $spatialDescription }

hitsSingleSlitEmerging.value: As more particles arrive, hits are beginning to concentrate in a broad region near the center of the screen, with fewer toward the edges.

hitsSingleSlitClear.value: A diffraction pattern has formed. Hits concentrate in a broad central band that tapers toward the edges, matching the continuous intensity pattern. { $spatialDescription }

rulerBands.value: { $numBands } bands centered at 0 millimeters, spaced roughly { $bandSpacing } millimeters apart.

rulerBandsSingleSlit.value: The central band spans roughly { $centralWidth } millimeters, centered at 0.

noRulerBands.value: { $numBands } bands centered on the screen, spread symmetrically to either side.

noRulerBandsSingleSlit.value: A broad central band centered on the screen, tapering toward the edges.

rulerPeaks.value: { $numPeaks } peaks centered at 0 millimeters, spaced roughly { $peakSpacing } millimeters apart.

rulerPeaksSingleSlit.value: The central peak spans roughly { $centralWidth } millimeters, centered at 0.

noRulerPeaks.value: { $numPeaks } peaks centered on the graph, spread symmetrically to either side.

noRulerPeaksSingleSlit.value: A broad central peak, tapering toward the edges.

accessibleHelpText.value: Clear all hits from the detector screen.
Capture the current detector screen pattern as a snapshot.
Open a dialog to compare saved snapshots.
